questions.question: Where does the text-RAG pipeline lose signal on visually rich PDFs?
options: Only at the final LLM step

At every stage: OCR drops chart data, 300-500-token chunking breaks table rows, multi-column layout flattens, figure annotations vanish, and one bi-encoder vector per chunk ignores the rest — five lossy steps
Text-RAG is lossless on PDFs
Only when the PDF is scanned
questions.explanation: A financial report's Q3 growth lives in a chart; a contract's signature block is a layout fact. ColPali's question: why extract text at all? Embed the page IMAGE directly and keep all of it.

questions.question: What is ColBERT's MaxSim operation?
options: Taking the max over document lengths
One vector PER TOKEN instead of per document; score = sum over query tokens of the max cosine similarity against any document token (Σᵢ maxⱼ cos(qᵢ, dⱼ)) — each query token "picks" its best match
Averaging all token embeddings first
A reranking LLM call per document
questions.explanation: Late interaction trades storage (N_d vectors per document) for strong recall and term-level semantics — the foundation ColPali generalizes from text tokens to image patches.

questions.question: How does ColPali apply the ColBERT pattern to document pages?
options: It OCRs the page then runs ColBERT

PaliGemma encodes each page image into patch embeddings (N_p vectors); the text query becomes token embeddings; MaxSim runs between query tokens and image patches, retrieving top-k pages by total score
It hashes page thumbnails
It asks a VLM to rank pages in one prompt
questions.explanation: Ingestion = embed every page, store all patch vectors. Query time = embed query tokens, MaxSim against stored pages. Each patch vector captures local layout AND content — no extraction step to lose either.

questions.question: What did ColPali's benchmarks show, and what is the main cost?
options: 5% better with no downsides
20-40% better end-to-end accuracy than text-RAG on visually rich documents — paid for in storage, since N_p patch vectors per page grow fast (mitigated by PQ/OPQ quantization)
Equal accuracy at lower cost
Better accuracy only on plain-text pages
questions.explanation: Multi-vector indexes are the price of late interaction. ColQwen2, ColSmol, and VisRAG extended the same pattern with different backbone VLMs.

questions.question: How do bi-encoder and late-interaction retrieval differ at query time?
options: They are mathematically identical

Bi-encoder compares ONE query vector to ONE document vector by cosine; late interaction keeps many vectors per side and computes fine-grained token/patch-level matching — costlier, but able to attend to specific regions
Bi-encoders cannot use cosine similarity
Late interaction requires no stored vectors
questions.explanation: A single pooled vector must compress an entire page into one point; MaxSim lets the query term "revenue chart" match the exact patch where the chart sits.
